query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1; live-reproduced on 8792: n=1 returned RemoveField.deconstruct@0.7904, base Field.deconstruct absent from top-5. Symbol identity is not a ranking signal.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.7: forced-inclusion of exact symbol-name matches into hybrid pool; Field.deconstruct now rank1 score=1.2. UPG-11.10: symbol displayed as qualified 'Field.deconstruct'.

query: from_db_value convert database value to python object on a model field
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1; query for from_db_value surfaced sibling get_db_prep_value. CORRECTED (F7/loop1 gate): base Field in fields/__init__.py has NO from_db_value method; the real implementation is JSONField.from_db_value at json.py:87. Expectation updated to target the correct file. UPDATED loop2: UPG-11.10 changed symbol display from bare 'from_db_value' to qualified 'JSONField.from_db_value'; updated expect.symbol accordingly. Actual rank3 now matches.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12: JSONField.from_db_value now rank3 score=0.9971. AreaField.from_db_value (rank1, GIS) and DistanceField.from_db_value (rank2, GIS) outrank it but all three are valid from_db_value impls. Top-3 contains JSONField.from_db_value in json.py.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1 sub-bug: displayed score is the STALE pre-rerank hybrid score; results not ordered by it (rank1=0.780 < rank5=0.798). Ship a real, monotonic ranking score regardless of F1.1.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.2: score is now the final post-rerank composite score; verified monotonic [1.2, 1.0124, 1.0051, 0.9978, 0.9905].

query: how to write a custom model field with deconstruct and from_db_value
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F2: 8792 indexed python/html/javascript/markdown ONLY. Django ships docs as .txt; docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt (the single best resource for the task) was invisible to search. Opus D found it via Glob+Read.
resolved: loop4 UPG-11.11: is_doc_intent_query() classifies 'how to ...' queries; on doc-intent, forced-inclusion suppressed AND doc-prose quality multiplier raised to 1.0 (from 0.70). docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt surfaces in top-5. F1/F1b/F4/F5/F7/F8 code-intent queries unregressed (forced-inclusion still active, doc-prose still 0.70).

query: understand the base Field class lifecycle and validation
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F3: comprehension queries return a 2-5k chunk; model re-read the 12-14k fields/__init__.py 4-8x/session because search offers no expand-to-full-symbol/file affordance. Encode as metric once the affordance exists; currently a known gap.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.4: symbol_start_line and symbol_end_line now present in /v1/search REST response. Verified: rank1 returns symbol_start_line=73 symbol_end_line=134.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: indexer stores symbol_name as bare leaf only (e.g. 'deconstruct', never 'Field.deconstruct'). The symbol_identity_boost qualified-match path (+0.20 for Class.method) is dead code at query time because re.split('[.:]+', symbol_name) always yields a single-element list. All deconstruct overrides get identical +0.10 leaf boost; discrimination is impossible. Fix: store symbol_name as 'Class.method' (using the class_context already tracked in _collect_chunks_ast at indexer.py:306) OR extract class prefix from content '# class: X' at query time in _apply_quality_and_dedup.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.10: symbol_name now stored/displayed as 'Class.method'; UPG-11.7 forced-inclusion lifts Field.deconstruct to rank1.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: Field.deconstruct (lines 606-705, ~100 lines with docstring) does not appear in the top-50 hybrid candidate pool for the F1 query. The boost is applied post-retrieval and cannot rescue a chunk that never entered the pool. Root cause: the long docstring diverges in embedding space from the short concrete query; BM25 scores are also low because keyword overlap is diluted. Fix options: (1) index the method signature+first-line as a separate short chunk, (2) expand vector fetch pool beyond _RERANK_TOP_K_UNFILTERED=60, (3) store symbol metadata for forced inclusion of exact-name matches.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.7: forced-inclusion unions all chunks whose symbol leaf matches a query token into the pool before rerank. Field.deconstruct now rank1.

query: list all database migrations for a project
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: symbol_identity_boost fires a +0.10 leaf boost for any symbol whose leaf is a common English word that appears in the query. Examples: leaf='all' boosted by 'list all migrations'; leaf='get' boosted by 'get a record'; leaf='set' boosted by 'set field value'. is_compound=False for single-word 3+ char leaves, so sub-word path fires on casual English use. No minimum-length guard or stop-word filter. Impact: can silently shift rankings on any query containing common English words that coincide with short method names.
resolved: loop1 f35dee1: stop-word + min-leaf-len(4) guard in symbol_identity_boost; verified GREEN over REST on django ('all' absent from top-5). Note: 4-letter gap tracked separately as F8. Note loop2: forced-inclusion now pulls LinearGeometryMixin.project to rank1 via 'project' (7 chars); 'all' absent check still passes but a NEW fault exists (tracked as F9).

query: from_db_value convert database value to python object on a model field
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: F1b expects 'Field.from_db_value' but the base Field class in django/db/models/fields/__init__.py has NO from_db_value method. The method only exists on JSONField (json.py:87), GIS fields (gis/db/models/sql/conversion.py), etc. F1b's expected file+symbol are wrong; the correct expectation is top-3 contains JSONField.from_db_value or similar concrete implementation. Actual rank1 is DatabaseOperations.convert_datefield_value (oracle) which is semantically unrelated. Both the test expectation and the ranking are wrong. UPDATED loop2: UPG-11.10 changed display to qualified form; corrected expected symbol to 'JSONField.from_db_value'. JSONField.from_db_value is now at rank3 (json.py) but harness checks top-3 WITH combined file+symbol. Passes the relaxed check but AreaField/DistanceField (GIS) outrank JSONField.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12: JSONField.from_db_value confirmed at rank3 score=0.9971 with file=json.py. Top-3 check passes. GIS fields outrank but are semantically valid implementations.

query: read lines from a text file
origin: loop2 adversarial: stop-word guard covers 3-letter-and-under leaves and a curated set of English stopwords, but 4-letter common English/programming words (read, join, hash, repr, open, eval, exec, iter, next, sort, copy, load, dump, emit, bind, call, send, wait) are NOT in prog_stopwords.txt and have len>=SYMBOL_MIN_LEAF_LEN=4. When these appear as natural-language verbs in a query (e.g. 'read lines from a text file' contains token 'read'), any symbol with leaf='read' gets +0.10 spurious boost. Confirmed: symbol_identity_boost('read', tokens)=0.1 for query 'read lines from a text file'. Impact: latent risk in code-heavy corpora (uv/rust/zig) where 'read', 'send', 'join', 'call' are common method names that compete with more relevant symbols. Django corpus shows the read method at rank7 with score 0.7106 boosted by +0.10 on this query.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.8: 'read' added to prog_stopwords.txt; verified 'read' leaf absent from top-3. rank1=HttpRequest.readlines (leaf='readlines', not 'read') which is correct behavior.

query: list all database migrations for a project
origin: loop2 adversarial: UPG-11.7 forced-inclusion uses _FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7 as the guard for non-compound tokens. Common English words of 7+ chars that also appear as method names cause forced-inclusion false positives. Example: 'project' (7 chars, no underscore) in 'list all database migrations for a project' triggers forced-inclusion of LinearGeometryMixin.project from django.contrib.gis.geos.geometry into the candidate pool. It receives sym_boost=+0.10 and final score=1.1 (rank1), beating Migration (rank2, 0.9344) and migration docs. Root cause: the 7-char threshold correctly targets identifiers like 'deconstruct' (11) and 'migrations' (10) but also captures prose words like 'project', 'message', 'execute', 'context', 'setting', 'response', 'process'. Impact: any query containing a common English word of 7+ chars that coincides with a method name risks having an unrelated symbol at rank1.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): BM25+vec-sim relevance gate for non-compound tokens. LinearGeometryMixin.project ABSENT from top-3; rank1=Migration score=0.95, rank2=Migration score=0.7833, rank3=docs/topics/migrations.txt score=0.7. Gate works: geometry chunk has near-zero BM25 overlap with migration/database terms minus 'project'.

query: connect to database and execute query
origin: loop2 adversarial: 'execute' (7 chars, no underscore) triggers forced-inclusion, pulling ALL .execute() methods into the pool with sym_boost. This floods top-4: BaseCommand.execute (rank1), ManagementUtility.execute (rank2), Command.execute sqlmigrate (rank3), Command.execute runserver (rank4). CursorWrapper.execute (the correct DB cursor answer) appears at rank7 only. Additionally 'connect' (7 chars) forces Signal.connect to rank5. The correct answer is demoted 6 ranks by an unrelated method-name collision.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): CursorWrapper.execute is rank1 score=1.1; BaseDatabaseWrapper.connect rank2 score=1.0841; BaseDatabaseSchemaEditor.execute rank3 score=1.0683. Both 'execute' and 'connect' candidates pass the relevance gate because db/backends chunks have strong BM25 overlap with 'database', 'query', 'connect' terms.

origin: loop2 adversarial: 'context' (7 chars) and 'template' (8 chars) both trigger forced-inclusion. DecimalField.context (a cached_property for decimal precision context) gets sym_boost=+0.10 and final score=1.1, landing at rank1 for 'render template context in view'. The correct answers (RequestContext, TemplateResponse, render_to_string) are absent from top-3. Root cause: same as F9 — the 7-char non-compound threshold captures common English words.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): DecimalField.context ABSENT from top-3; rank1=PartialTemplate.render score=1.1, rank2=BaseRenderer.render score=1.0833, rank3=TemplateView.get score=0.9667. Decimal context chunk fails relevance gate: near-zero BM25 overlap with 'render','template','view' minus 'context'.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop2 adversarial: displayed scores regularly exceed 1.0 (e.g., Field.deconstruct=1.2, BloomIndex.deconstruct=1.0124). The scoring formula is base_rank(1.0 for rank1) * quality + sym_boost(0.1 or 0.2), which is not bounded at [0,1]. A user seeing score=1.2 has no natural interpretation — scores are supposed to indicate relevance similarity. This is an ergonomics/UX issue: the score field convention from vector similarity [0,1] is violated. Impact: callers who use score as a threshold (e.g., 'only use if score>0.8') get false signals; scores are monotonic but unnormalized. Note: sorted_by_score remains true so ranking correctness is unaffected; this is purely a display/contract issue.

query: save a model instance to the database
origin: loop3 adversarial false-negative attack: 'save' is 4 chars, no underscore — excluded from forced-inclusion (correct by min_identifier_len=7 guard). Model.save in django/db/models/base.py:841 does NOT appear in top-50 results for any natural-language formulation of 'save a model'. Rank1 is always ModelAdmin.save_model (admin helper, not the ORM method) because save_model is a compound name that semantically matches 'save'+'model' well in the embedding space. Model.save only surfaces when queried with code-syntax tokens like 'force_insert force_update update_fields'. User impact: a developer asking how to save a model instance in Django will be directed to the admin helper rather than the core ORM method — the single most-used Django API is invisible to natural-language search. Root cause: 4-char common verbs ('save','get','set','add') below forced-inclusion threshold AND lose the regular hybrid to compound-named admin variants that score higher on vector similarity. This is a pre-existing limitation not introduced by UPG-11.12.

query: exclude certain records from a queryset
origin: loop3 adversarial: 'queryset' (8 chars, not a stop word) passes the forced-inclusion guard and force-includes ALL methods named 'queryset' (10 ListFilter.queryset() variants in django). These pass the BM25+vec-sim relevance gate because filter-context chunks have some keyword overlap. Result: ListFilter.queryset ranks1-3 (scores 1.013, 0.9986, 0.9841), QuerySet.exclude is demoted to rank4 (score 0.9826). The word 'queryset' in the query is used as a NOUN (the container) but treated by forced-inclusion as a method-name target. Same pattern as F9/F11 but the relevance gate fails to exclude ListFilter.queryset because those chunks DO discuss filtering querysets (BM25/vec-sim both pass). Root cause: 'queryset' should be in the forced-inclusion prose stop-word list alongside 'context', 'project' etc. Impact: any natural-language query using 'queryset' as a noun (very common in Django queries) risks having the genuinely correct ORM method (exclude, filter, values, annotate) pushed off rank1-3 by ListFilter admin variants.
